mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5d return volatility exceeds 2.5% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer price inflation (CPI) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand pressures that push central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between CPI and HOUST drops below 0.4 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates expanding real economic activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or INDPRO growth falls below 0.5% in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening of monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) creates a 'stagflation' risk that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the FEDFUNDS-CPI spread narrows below 1.0% in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls > 2% MoM and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the RSAFS-UNRATE correlation drops below -0.3 in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation, prompting central banks to raise rates and depress equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or UNRATE fails to decline further in subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) alongside a decline in capacity utilization (INDPRO relative to peak) suggests overheating manufacturing sectors that trigger policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 2% MoM and exceeds 95% of historical peak
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or INDPRO growth falls below 1% in subsequent months.
